betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6ME Chest§f:
The ME Chest (appliedenergistics2:chest) is the §eportable equivalent of an ME Drive§f. It is a single-cell storage device that combines the cell and housing into one block.

§6Crafting Recipe (network/blocks/chest.json)§f:
- 1× Quartz Glass (appliedenergistics2:quartz_glass) at center
- 1× Fluix Crystal (appliedenergistics2:material:7) or Pure Fluix Crystal
- 1× Chest (vanilla) at center
- 4× Iron Ingot (vanilla, in corners)
- 1× Logic Processor (appliedenergistics2:material:22)
- Yields 1× ME Chest

§6Function§f:
The ME Chest:
- §bStores up to 1 ME Storage Cell§f internally
- §bProvides item I/O via top face§f: insert items from above, extract from below
- §bExposes cell contents to the network§f: the cell is treated as part of the network's storage
- §bHas a small GUI§f: shows cell fill level and allows manual item insertion/extraction
- §bActs as a network node§f: can be used as a cable endpoint

§6Direct Descendants of ME Chest (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 2 items that use the ME Chest:
- ME Security Terminal (appliedenergistics2:security_station) — security-locked terminal
- Portable Cell (appliedenergistics2:portable_cell) — handheld version of the chest

§6ME Chest vs ME Drive§f:
- §bME Chest§f: 1 cell, no hot-swap, lower cost, has a GUI
- §bME Drive§f: 10 cells, hot-swap, no GUI, requires external item I/O via Interfaces
- §bME Chest§f is good for small networks; ME Drive for large ones

§6Crafting Ingredients (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 8 ingredient items in the ME Chest recipe:
- Glass (vanilla, central column)
- 7× Stained Glass variants (Orange, Magenta, Light Blue, Yellow, Lime, Pink, Gray) for the chest sides — all map to the same Chest tag in OreDict

§6Why It Matters§f:
The ME Chest is the §esimplest networked storage device§f in AE2. It is perfect for:
- §bEarly-game networks§f: one chest + one cell + one controller is enough
- §bSingle-player casual play§f: no need for a full Drive setup
- §bPortable cell precursor§f: combined with a Portable Cell housing, gives handheld ME access

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30149 (Printed Circuits & Processors) — the ME Chest requires a Logic Processor.

§eTip§f: For a quick early-game network, place a Controller + ME Chest + Energy Acceptor. This is the minimum viable ME setup.

§eTip§f: Use the §bME Chest's top face for input§f (auto-feed from above) and §bbottom face for output§f. This is the default item flow direction.

§cWarning§f: The ME Chest has a single fixed cell slot. To upgrade cell capacity, you must break the chest (recovering the cell) and craft a new one — there is no cell hot-swap like the ME Drive.
